title: Pattern Governance
description: Schema for pattern-tier governance rules — rules for a single-page/single-surface composition (e.g. Card's footer). Unlike journeys, patterns don't inherit from a shared family; each pattern owns its rules outright. Zones replace steps as the unit provisions attach to.

$schema.description: Path to this JSON Schema for IDE validation and autocomplete.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier for this pattern (e.g. 'card').

scope.description: Scope code that prefixes every rule id in this file (e.g. 'CRD').

description.description: What this pattern represents and why it needs pattern-level (not component-level) governance.

zones.description: Named regions of the pattern's single surface, referenced by rule provisions for human-readable labels.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier for this zone (e.g. 'footer').

rules.description: The pattern's governance rules — each a citable rule, validated holistically against the full composition of a zone.

aiHints.description: Hints for AI agents consuming this pattern.

keywords.description: Terms that trigger this pattern's rules during intent matching. Become the rules' triggers in the generated index.

notes.description: Natural language guidance for AI agents on how to apply these rules holistically.

severity.description: How hard the rule pushes back. error = hard block; warning = flagged for review; info = advisory.

ruleId.description: Authored, stable rule citation (e.g. 'CRD-1'). Never reused once retired.

title.description: Short human sentence naming the rule.

refines.description: Id of another rule this rule supersedes (lex specialis), if any.

statement.description: The binding text of the rule.

rationale.description: Why the rule exists — the prose justification.

appliesWhen.description: The trigger / jurisdiction. Common keys: pattern, zone, always.

provisions.description: The same rule applied to specific zones (the decimal citation, e.g. CRD-1.1).

status.description: Mark a rule repealed: it stays in the authored YAML (its citation is retired, never reused) but is dropped from the generated governance index.

zone.description: Id of the zone this provision applies to.

labelHint.description: Suggested label text. Informational only — not enforced.

type.description: required = must be present; forbidden = must not appear; limit = bounded count (use min/max).

variant.description: The specific variant this constraint targets. Omit to apply to all variants.

min.description: Minimum instance count. Omit for no minimum.

max.description: Maximum instance count. Omit for no maximum.
